properties.name: Round Top Heavy Rare Earth Deposit
properties.operator: Texas Mineral Resources / USA Rare Earth
properties.description: Heavy rare earth and critical minerals deposit at Round Top Mountain near Sierra Blanca.
properties.sourceName: Texas Mineral Resources Corp. - Round Top Mountain
properties.notes: Coordinate follows Round Top prospects locality; project area is larger than point.

properties.name: Little Round Top Prospects
properties.description: Prospect area adjacent to the Round Top REE system in the Sierra Blanca Peaks.
properties.sourceName: Mindat - Round Top, Hudspeth County, Texas
properties.notes: Coordinate from Mindat locality listing; not a public access point.

properties.name: Little Blanca Mountain Prospects
properties.description: Rare-metal/critical-minerals prospect area within the Sierra Blanca Peaks mineralized system.
properties.sourceName: Mindat - Round Top, Hudspeth County, Texas
properties.notes: Coordinate from Mindat locality listing; representative of prospect area.

properties.name: Sierra Blanca Peaks Rare-Metal District
properties.description: District-scale record for the Sierra Blanca Peaks rare-earth, beryllium, uranium, fluorine, and related mineralization.
properties.sourceName: Bureau of Economic Geology - Rare Earth Elements and Critical Minerals
properties.notes: Representative point for a district, not a mine entrance or lease boundary.

properties.name: Trans-Pecos Rare Earth Laccolith Trend
properties.description: Representative record for Trans-Pecos laccoliths and related igneous systems evaluated for rare earth and critical-mineral potential.
properties.sourceName: Bureau of Economic Geology - Rare Earth Elements and Critical Minerals
properties.notes: Regional trend point; useful for atlas context only.

properties.name: Llano Uplift Rare Earth Pegmatite Region
properties.description: Central Texas Llano Uplift region containing historical REE-bearing pegmatites, including Baringer Hill and associated localities.
properties.sourceName: USGS - Rare metals, Bulletin 340
properties.notes: Regional centroid for Llano/Burnet rare-earth pegmatite context.

properties.name: Texas Gulf Coastal Plain Lignite REE Potential Region
properties.description: Representative region for REE and critical-mineral potential in Gulf Coastal Plain lignites.
properties.sourceName: USGS - Rare earth and critical elements in Texas Gulf Coastal Plain lignites
properties.notes: Representative research-region point for lignite feedstock potential.

properties.name: Texas Coal Ash REE Potential Resource Region
properties.description: Representative point for Texas coal ash as a possible REE and critical-minerals feedstock.
properties.sourceName: Bureau of Economic Geology - Critical Minerals and Rare Earth Elements in Texas
properties.notes: Point represents statewide/Gulf Coast coal-ash potential, not one ash pond.

properties.name: Round Top State Lease Area
properties.operator: Texas Mineral Resources / USA Rare Earth
properties.description: State lease area encompassing Round Top and adjacent project lands for REE and critical-mineral development.
properties.sourceName: SEC filing - Round Top project lease description
properties.notes: Same general point as Round Top because public lease description gives acreage, not polygon coordinates.

properties.name: Round Top Northwest Prospecting Permit Area
properties.operator: Texas Mineral Resources / USA Rare Earth
properties.description: Representative point for prospecting-permit acreage adjacent to the Round Top state leases.
properties.sourceName: Texas Mineral Resources Corp. - Round Top Mountain
properties.notes: Representative point within adjacent prospecting-permit context; not a boundary.

properties.name: Round Top East Prospecting Permit Area
properties.operator: Texas Mineral Resources / USA Rare Earth
properties.description: Representative eastern prospecting area connected to the Round Top critical-minerals project context.
properties.sourceName: Texas Mineral Resources Corp. - Round Top Mountain
properties.notes: Approximate representative point; use project polygons if available later.

properties.name: Round Top Heap-Leach Mine Plan Area
properties.operator: Texas Mineral Resources / USA Rare Earth
properties.description: Representative point for the proposed Round Top mining and heap-leach development concept.
properties.sourceName: Texas Mineral Resources Corp. - Round Top Mountain
properties.notes: Project-plan point, not construction boundary.

properties.name: Quitman Mountains Critical Minerals Appraisal Area
properties.description: Representative record for Quitman Mountains/Trans-Pecos critical-mineral appraisal context near the Round Top system.
properties.sourceName: Bureau of Economic Geology - Rare Earth Elements and Critical Minerals
properties.notes: Low coordinate precision; included for regional geologic context only.

properties.name: Badu Hill REE Pegmatite Appraisal Area
properties.description: REE-bearing pegmatite locality historically associated with rare-earth and uranium/thorium minerals.
properties.sourceName: Mindat - Badu Hill quarry
properties.notes: Coordinate from nearby locality listing; not a public access point.

properties.name: Kingsland/Petrick Pegmatite Appraisal Area
properties.description: Petrick/Kingsland pegmatite locality with rare-earth minerals including gadolinite-bearing material.
properties.sourceName: Mindat - Kingsland Granite Company Quarry / Petrick Quarry
properties.notes: Coordinate from Mindat; included as historical/appraisal locality.

properties.name: East Texas Lignite REE Sampling Area
properties.description: Representative East Texas lignite area for REE and critical-mineral sampling in the Gulf Coastal Plain.
properties.sourceName: USGS - Rare earth and critical elements in Texas Gulf Coastal Plain lignites
properties.notes: Representative East Texas lignite sampling point; not a mine permit point.

properties.name: South Texas Lignite REE Sampling Area
properties.description: Representative South Texas lignite area for possible REE-bearing coal and related feedstock evaluation.
properties.sourceName: USGS - Rare earth and critical elements in Texas Gulf Coastal Plain lignites
properties.notes: Representative research-area point.

properties.name: Gulf Coast Basin Coal-Ash REE Research Area
properties.description: Representative Gulf Coast Basin record for coal ash, refuse, lignite and related feedstocks evaluated for REEs and critical minerals.
properties.sourceName: NETL - Assessment of REE and critical minerals in Gulf Coast Basin feedstocks
properties.notes: Regional research point; not an active recovery facility.

properties.name: MP Materials Independence Magnet Manufacturing Facility
properties.description: Fort Worth rare-earth magnetics facility producing NdFeB magnetics from rare-earth materials.
properties.sourceName: MP Materials - Facilities
properties.notes: Address-based point for 13840 Independence Parkway; coordinates are approximate facility centroid.

properties.name: MP Materials Independence Metallization Facility
properties.description: Rare-earth metallization capability at MP Materials' Independence site in Fort Worth.
properties.sourceName: MP Materials - Independence
properties.notes: Same industrial campus as Independence magnet facility; represented separately because it is a distinct supply-chain stage.

properties.name: MP Materials / Apple Recycled Magnet Production Line
properties.description: Planned recycled rare-earth magnet output from the Independence facility under Apple's 2025 supply-chain commitment.
properties.sourceName: Apple Newsroom - Apple and MP Materials partnership
properties.notes: Same Fort Worth site; record represents recycled-magnet line/supply-chain function.

properties.name: MP Materials 10X Northlake Magnet Campus
properties.description: Announced rare-earth magnet manufacturing campus in Northlake intended to expand U.S. NdFeB magnet capacity.
properties.sourceName: MP Materials investor release - 10X Northlake campus
properties.notes: Representative point near Northlake industrial area; public materials identify Northlake but not a final parcel centroid in accessible text.

properties.name: Noveon Magnetics San Marcos Facility
properties.description: San Marcos magnet-to-magnet manufacturing and rare-earth magnet recycling facility.
properties.sourceName: Noveon Magnetics - Contact
properties.notes: Address-based point for 1550 Clovis Barker Road; coordinate approximate.

properties.name: Noveon M2M Magnet Recycling Operation
properties.description: Recycling-focused record for Noveon's M2M rare-earth magnet recovery and remanufacturing process.
properties.sourceName: StopWaste vendor listing - Noveon Magnetics San Marcos
properties.notes: Same San Marcos facility as Noveon manufacturing record; separate record reflects recycling role.

properties.name: Momentum Technologies Carrollton R&D Facility
properties.description: Carrollton R&D facility focused on recycling and recovery of critical minerals and rare earth metals.
properties.sourceName: Momentum Technologies - Contact
properties.notes: Address-based point for 1625 W. Crosby Road, Suite 124; coordinate approximate.

properties.name: ElementUSA Critical Resource Accelerator
properties.description: Cedar Park facility focused on critical-mineral recovery, waste-feedstock testing, and process acceleration.
properties.sourceName: ElementUSA - Critical Resource Accelerator launch
properties.notes: Cedar Park facility point; exact address not public in source, so coordinate is city/industrial-area representative.

properties.name: ElementUSA Gallium-Scandium Demonstration Development
properties.description: Texas-based initial development work for gallium and scandium recovery demonstration connected to ElementUSA's Critical Resource Accelerator.
properties.sourceName: Department of War - ElementUSA gallium and scandium award
properties.notes: Same Cedar Park CRA context; final demonstration facility details may differ.

properties.name: Bureau of Economic Geology Critical Minerals Research
properties.operator: Bureau of Economic Geology, UT Austin
properties.description: Research site for Texas rare-earth and critical-mineral assessment work, including Round Top and feedstock studies.
properties.sourceName: Bureau of Economic Geology - Rare Earth Elements and Critical Minerals
properties.notes: Institutional point for BEG/Jackson School campus area in Austin.

properties.name: Kingsland Granite Company Quarry (Petrick Quarry)
properties.description: Historical quarry and pegmatite locality associated with rare-earth minerals including gadolinite and britholite-type occurrences.
properties.sourceName: Mindat - Kingsland Granite Company Quarry / Petrick Quarry
properties.notes: Coordinate from Mindat locality page; not a public access invitation.

properties.name: Houston Industrial Byproduct Critical Minerals Node
properties.description: Representative Houston Ship Channel/petrochemical-complex node for critical-mineral recovery from industrial waste and byproducts.
properties.sourceName: Texas Tribune - rare minerals/mining rush in Texas
properties.notes: Not a single confirmed rare-earth plant; included for supply-chain context.

properties.name: Permian Industrial Waste Critical Minerals Recovery Node
properties.description: Representative Permian Basin node for critical-mineral recovery interest from oilfield and industrial waste streams.

properties.name: Round Top Proposed On-Site Separation Plant

properties.notes: Project-plan point; not a completed operating plant.

properties.notes: Coordinate from Mindat map; site is submerged and not accessible as a mine.

properties.name: Rare-Earth Mine, Burnet County

properties.sourceName: Mindat - Rare-Earth Mine, Burnet County
properties.notes: Approximate point inferred from Mindat distance relation near Baringer Hill; not a public access point.

properties.name: Badu Hill Quarry
properties.description: REE-U-Th-bearing pegmatite locality historically known as Badu Hill quarry/mine.
properties.sourceName: Mindat - Badu Hill quarry
properties.notes: Coordinate from Mindat locality listing.

properties.name: Clear Creek Pegmatite
